mncPlinesEdit.Description: Collection of various plugins for working with polylines
mncPlinesEdit.h7: The ModPlus tab is not found
mncPlinesEdit.h8: The ModPlus tab was not found to add a work panel to polylines
mncPlinesEdit.h13: Divide the arc by an equal number of segments\nThe image shows a variant of dividing by 3 segments\nR is the arc radius in the polyline
mncPlinesEdit.h14: Divide the arc into segments by specifying the length of the arc segments resulting from the division\nR is the arc radius in the polyline\nL – length of the arc segment
mncPlinesEdit.h15: Divide the arc into segments by specifying the chord deviation (segment height)\nR is the arc radius in the polyline\nH – segment height
mncPlinesEdit.h16: Divide the arc into segments by specifying the length of the chord\nR is the arc radius in the polyline\nX – chord length
mncPlinesEdit.h22: P is the point of the polyline
mncPlinesEdit.h23: - Plugin works by checking both conditions
mncPlinesEdit.h24: H is the maximum deviation from the straight line:
mncPlinesEdit.err1: There are no blocks available for insertion in the document
mncPlinesEdit.cb5: by insertion point of the block
mncPlinesEdit.d1: Create copies of selected 3D polylines as LW-polylines (2D) with transfer to level 0.0
mncPlinesEdit.d2: Deleting adjacent vertices of selected polylines that have the same coordinates
mncPlinesEdit.l3: Removing vertices that lie on a straight line
mncPlinesEdit.d3: Deleting adjacent vertices of selected polyline, which lie on a straight line. It is possible to set tolerance for deviation from the straight or angular tolerance
mncPlinesEdit.l4: Placing an object at the vertices of a polyline
mncPlinesEdit.d4: Place the selected object in the vertices of the polyline. It is possible to rotate the object along the polyline segment. Blocks can be placed both on the geometric center, and on the insertion point
mncPlinesEdit.l5: Replacing an arc segment with linear
mncPlinesEdit.d5: Replacing the specified arc segment of the polyline with a linear
mncPlinesEdit.l6: Replacing an linear segment with arc
mncPlinesEdit.d6: Replacing the specified linear (or arc) segment of the polyline with an arc. It is possible to construct an arc along a tangent or a point on an arc
mncPlinesEdit.d7: Dynamically adding a vertex to the specified polyline
mncPlinesEdit.l8: Drawing a rectangle by three points
mncPlinesEdit.l9: Removing Arc Segments from a Polyline
mncPlinesEdit.d9: Remove polyline arc segments by replacing them with linear segments. There are several options for the plugin: the number of segments, the length of segments, the height of segments (chord deviation), the length of the chord
mncPlinesEdit.d10: The construction of a middle line (as polyline) between two specified curves (segments, polylines or splines)
mncPlinesEdit.d11: Fast moving of vertices of the specified polyline
mncPlinesEdit.d12: Split curve (segment, polyline or spline) at intersection points with other drawing objects
mncPlinesEdit.d13: Creates a quadrilateral from two or more points. The sides of the quadrilateral are oriented according to the current coordinate system. An angular tolerance setting is available allowing the sides to capture a greater number of points.
mncPlinesEdit.d14: Creates objects at intersection points of curves with specified objects
mncPlinesEdit.undividable: An error occurred while dividing the curve. Try reducing the curve length
mncPlinesEdit.k8: The maximum deviation from the straight line (H) is accepted
mncPlinesEdit.k12: does not contain vertices lying on a straight line or suitable under the conditions of tolerance
mncPlinesEdit.k15: Select an arc segment of the polyline
mncPlinesEdit.k17: Specify a point on the tangent or: [Tangent/traverse Point]
mncPlinesEdit.k18: Specify a point on the arc or: [Tangent/traverse Point]
mncPlinesEdit.k19: Specify the point (hold Ctrl to move the vertex forward)
mncPlinesEdit.k24: Enter the length of the segment
mncPlinesEdit.k26: Enter the length of the chord
mncPlinesEdit.k27: Select first support element (line, polyline or spline)
mncPlinesEdit.k28: Select second support element (line, polyline or spline)
mncPlinesEdit.k31: Select a curve (segment, polyline or spline)
mncPlinesEdit.k32: Specify a point on the tangent or:
mncPlinesEdit.k35: Specify a point on the arc or:
mncPlinesEdit.k38: Select one or more curves (line, polyline, arc, spline or circle)
mncPlinesEdit.k39: Select objects for intersection calculation or leave the selection empty to calculate for all objects
